_comment: Trivial smoke-test milestone for scripts/milestone-runner.sh (ADR 0004/0006). This example intentionally uses Sonnet and the strict profile for a trivial task; a REAL milestone must use the strong model (claude-opus-4-8) — the session IS the orchestrator (discipline rule 10) — and long-running milestones should set permission_profile to 'auto' (classifier + OS sandbox: no per-command allowlisting; see ADR 0006) with sandbox_allowed_domains for any registries the build needs. In strict profile: widen allowed_tools to what the work needs — 'Agent' (subagent spawning, scopable as Agent(search)) and 'Skill' (scopable as Skill(orchestration)) are the verified tool names for delegation — and allowlist your gates' own harmless commands (here Bash(test:*)/Bash(wc:*)/Bash(echo:*)): a doctrine-following session WILL self-verify its acceptance criteria, and an out-of-allowlist attempt fails a strict run. Make both gates real commands (build/test), not existence probes. Calibration (measured live): a session that loads a full steering layer costs ~$0.25 before any work — set the per-iteration cap well above that floor or the CLI kills the session mid-boot.
goal: HELLO.md exists at the repo root with a one-line greeting and a second line describing this repo in one sentence.
